meta.source: termmux deep autopsy: fix all review-*.md findings + scope expansion
meta.maintained: Takumi must update this file after EVERY sub-task. Deviations logged within the plan.
meta.reviewGateCycle: Each task: implement -> automated tests (full coverage) -> ALL platform checks pass (macOS, Linux container, Windows) -> lint pass (vet, staticcheck, deadcode) -> Rule of Two (two contiguous PASS reviews on identical diff via /strict-review-gate) -> commit -> update this blueprint -> next task. NO EXCEPTIONS.

globalAlerts.warning: CROSS-PLATFORM MANDATE: ALL checks must pass on ALL platforms. Run gmake cross-build for cross-compilation.
DEADCODE MANDATE: gmake deadcode must pass with ZERO unreachable functions.
LINT MANDATE: gmake lint (vet + staticcheck + deadcode) must pass after EVERY code change.
TDD DISCIPLINE: Write failing test FIRST, then implement the fix. A test that doesn't fail before the fix is not a valid test.
STRICT REVIEW GATE CYCLE (MANDATORY): Two contiguous PASS reviews on EXACT SAME diff via /strict-review-gate before ANY commit.

REVIEW EXCLUSION: Skip all findings that concern 'breaking API changes' or 'breaking API changes' — all Go code is internal-only. Dirty field removal, API contract breakage, exported field changes are EXCLUDED from this round.

mandatoryDirectives.contextRefresh: Read blueprint.json and WIP.md at session start. Resume from first non-Done task.
mandatoryDirectives.incrementalUpdate: Update task status after EVERY sub-task. Never batch updates.
mandatoryDirectives.noEstimates: Perfection isn't cheap — no effort estimates allowed.
mandatoryDirectives.noPriorities: All tasks are mandatory — no prioritization.
mandatoryDirectives.tddDiscipline: Every fix requires a failing test first.
mandatoryDirectives.crossPlatform: After every code change, verify: (1) local build+test, (2) gmake lint, (3) gmake deadcode.

statusSection.currentState: REPLAN-05: 7 review files analyzed, 26 findings extracted, 18 actionable (6 excluded as breaking-change concerns). Baseline: 7 commits on wip branch. Tasks 1-7 remain Done. Inserting review-fix tasks 8R1-8R18 into the pipeline before cross-platform verification and scope expansion.
statusSection.goalState: ALL 18 review-fix tasks pass strict-review-gate individually and are committed. Cross-platform verification passes on all platforms. Full suite passes on macOS, Linux, Windows. Blueprint never goes empty — scope always expands toward True Perfection.

Cross-referenced every finding against current code. Result: 18 actionable findings requiring fixes, 6 excluded (breaking API changes per user directive), 2 deferred (architectural concerns). Inserted 18 fix tasks before cross-platform verification.

8R1: Fix render_raster.go — NUL byte wide-char ambiguity + cellH=1 foreground threshold + dead bounds check + f.Close() error swallowing
sequentialTasks.description: REVIEW-1 #1 #2 #3 + REVIEW-2 #1. File: internal/termmux/vt/render_raster.go. FOUR fixes in one diff: (1) NUL sentinel: add a wideChar field to Cell or use an alternate sentinel check. Simplest fix: check cell.Ch > 0 (only NUL/rune zero is 0) but also verify the preceding cell is actually wide. Better approach: store wide-char flag in Attr or use a separate Wide field. For now, the most minimal fix: verify cell.Ch == 0 AND the previous cell is a valid wide character by checking uniseg width. Actually, the best minimal fix: skip cell.Ch == 0 ONLY when preceded by a cell with a known wide character. Since we can't easily store this, we can check: if scr.Cells[row][col].Ch == 0, check if col>0 AND scr.Cells[row][col-1].Ch is a known wide char (via uniseg.StringWidth(string(scr.Cells[row][col-1].Ch))). But that's expensive. Alternative: make Cell have a field indicating wide-char placeholder. Simplest correct approach that doesn't change the Cell struct: use the invariant that screen.go/PutChar writes the actual character in the first cell and NUL in the second — the only way a cell gets Ch==0 is as a wide-char placeholder. To defend against literal NUL bytes, check that the preceding cell has a wide character: if cell.Ch == 0 && col > 0 { prev := scr.Cells[row][col-1]; if !isWideRune(prev.Ch) { /* don't skip, treat as normal */ } }. Implement isWideRune via uniseg. (2) cellH=1: in the content rendering loop, compute threshold := max(1, cellH*3/4) — ensures at least 1 foreground pixel when hasContent. (3) Dead bounds check: remove the `if px >= imgW || py >= imgH { continue }` block — it's mathematically impossible to trigger. (4) f.Close() error: in SaveRasterPNG, capture the Close error. Write: `defer func() { if cerr := f.Close(); cerr != nil && err == nil { err = cerr } }()` or explicitly call f.Close() and check error before returning. Tests: add TestRaster_NULByteSentinel (put a NUL in a cell and verify it doesn't trigger wide-char skip), TestRaster_CellH1 (verify content cells render foreground with cellH=1), TestSaveRasterPNG_CloseError (mock or skip — the correctness is structural). TDD: write these tests first, confirm they fail, then implement fixes.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: /strict-review-gate produces two contiguous PASS reviews. Both confirm: (1) NUL byte in screen doesn't cause wide-char rendering corruption — a genuine NUL renders as its own cell (or empty cell), not as a wide-char placeholder, (2) cellH=1 content cells have visible foreground pixels (not pure background), (3) bounds checks removed without regression — all existing raster tests pass, (4) SaveRasterPNG properly propagates f.Close() errors, (5) gmake lint and gmake deadcode pass, (6) all existing render_raster_test.go tests still pass, (7) new tests pass.

sequentialTasks.task: 8R2: Fix statusbar — tighten assertions (exact match) + add SetTitle to concurrent test + positive fallback assertion
sequentialTasks.description: REVIEW-3 #1 #3 #4. File: internal/termmux/statusbar/statusbar_test.go. THREE fixes: (1) Add SetTitle goroutine to TestStatusBar_ConcurrentAccess — add a new goroutine calling sb.SetTitle(fmt.Sprintf("title-%d", i)) and increment the goroutines count from 8 to 9 to include it. (2) Tighten TestSetTitle and TestRender_NoTitleByDefault assertions from strings.Contains to exact substring match. For TestSetTitle: assert the full rendered text segment contains ` [myapp] ready │`. For TestRender_NoTitleByDefault: assert the rendered output contains ` ready │` (the no-title fallback) and does NOT contain `[` at the start position. (3) Add positive assertion to TestRender_NoTitleByDefault: after verifying no [Claude], also verify that the correct fallback output is present: strings.Contains(got, " ready │ Ctrl+] to switch "). TDD: write the failing tests first (ConcurrentAccess should fail -race, but adding SetTitle won't break current code — it just adds coverage).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: /strict-review-gate produces two contiguous PASS reviews. Both confirm: (1) TestStatusBar_ConcurrentAccess includes SetTitle goroutine, passes with -race, (2) TestSetTitle uses exact substring assertion — verifies " [myapp] ready │ Ctrl+] to switch " present, (3) TestRender_NoTitleByDefault asserts BOTH the absence of [Claude] AND the presence of " ready │ Ctrl+] to switch ", (4) All existing statusbar tests pass, (5) gmake lint and gmake deadcode pass.

sequentialTasks.task: 8R3: Fix pty — clone data in writeWithGoroutineTimeout to prevent data race
sequentialTasks.description: REVIEW-4 #1. File: internal/termmux/pty/pty.go, function writeWithGoroutineTimeout (line 143-158). Fix: clone the data slice before the goroutine write. Current code passes mutable data []byte directly to f.Write(data) in a background goroutine. After timeout, caller can mutate the slice while kernel reads it. Fix: add `bufCopy := make([]byte, len(data)); copy(bufCopy, data)` before the goroutine, and use bufCopy in the goroutine. TDD: write TestProcess_WriteGoroutineTimeout_NoWriteAfterTimeout that: (1) creates a Process with a small writeTimeout, (2) calls Write with a known byte slice, (3) after the timeout error is returned (or before), mutates the original byte slice, (4) verifies via mock or race detector that there's no data race. Alternatively, a simpler approach: use a mock *os.File (or pipe/fifo where writes block) to verify the goroutine uses a copy. Since we can't easily mock os.File, use -race with a goroutine that modifies the buffer after Write returns. TDD: write TestProcess_WriteTimeoutNoDataRace that sends a []byte to a blocking PTY, awaits the timeout, then modifies the original buffer, runs with -race.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: /strict-review-gate produces two contiguous PASS reviews. Both confirm: (1) writeWithGoroutineTimeout clones data before background goroutine write, (2) TestProcess_WriteTimeoutNoDataRace passes with -race flag, (3) all existing PTY tests pass, (4) gmake lint and gmake deadcode pass.

sequentialTasks.task: 8R4: Fix ptyio — reinstate copy in ReadLoop to prevent memory bloat
sequentialTasks.description: REVIEW-4 #2. File: internal/termmux/ptyio/reader.go, function ReadLoop (line 42-61). Fix: reinstate the chunk copy. The current code sends buf[:n] directly, pinning the 32KB backing array for every chunk in the channel. For 100 small reads, that's 3.2MB pinned. Fix: add `chunk := make([]byte, n); copy(chunk, buf[:n])` before sending to the channel, send `chunk` instead of `buf[:n]`. Update the comment accordingly. Note: the comment at line 49-51 claims "Since buf is freshly allocated each iteration, we can send buf[:n] directly" — this is misleading. The sender doesn't reuse buf, but the RECEIVER holds the slice, pinning the 32KB array. The correct optimization is to pre-allocate a pool or use a ring buffer, but for correctness, we must copy. TDD: write TestBufferedReader_NoLargeArrayPinning that verifies small reads don't pin large arrays. Since we can't directly test GC behavior, write a test that sends many small chunks through the reader and verifies the channel consumer receives correctly-sized slices (not 32KB). Actually, check: when we receive from the channel, len(chunk) should equal the original n, not 32KB. But we can already verify this by checking cap(). Test: create a BufferedReader with a reader that returns 1 byte, read 10 chunks, verify each chunk has cap == 1 (or <= n) — but this is hard to test because cap() reflects the underlying array. Better: write a test that sends 1000 small chunks and verifies no OOM or excessive allocs using testing.AllocsPerRun or just verify len() of each received chunk matches the sent size.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: /strict-review-gate produces two contiguous PASS reviews. Both confirm: (1) ReadLoop copies buf[:n] into a new slice before sending to channel, (2) existing ptyio tests pass, (3) gmake lint and gmake deadcode pass, (4) the comment about "freshly allocated each iteration" is updated to reflect the actual concern (receiver holds the backing array).

sequentialTasks.task: 8R5: Fix sgrmouse — differentiate incomplete vs malformed SGR sequences (stream poisoning prevention)
sequentialTasks.description: REVIEW-5 #1. File: internal/termmux/sgrmouse.go, function filterMouseForStatusBar (line 167-211). The critical bug: when parseSGRMouse returns ok=false, the code unconditionally treats it as an incomplete (truncated) sequence and returns buf[i:] as partial. If the sequence is malformed (e.g., \x1b[<INVALID), this causes stream poisoning where every subsequent read gets prepended with the malformed bytes, growing indefinitely. Fix: parseSGRMouse must differentiate between 'incomplete at buffer boundary' and 'malformed/not an SGR sequence'. Since parseSGRMouse already returns consumed=0 and ok=false for both cases, we need to add a mechanism to distinguish. Options: (A) Add a new return value from parseSGRMouse indicating incomplete vs malformed, (B) Check if we consumed any bytes past the ESC [ < prefix — if consumed > 3 (the prefix), it means we started parsing parameters but ran out of buffer, which is truly incomplete. If consumed == 0 but we matched the prefix, we need to scan forward for the terminating M/m byte to decide. Simplest robust fix: before treating as partial, scan forward from buf[i] to find if there's a complete SGR sequence further in the buffer. If no terminating M/m is found within buf[i:], it could be incomplete — buffer it. If a terminating byte IS found but the parse still fails, it's malformed — skip past it. Actually, the cleanest fix: in filterMouseForStatusBar, when parseSGRMouse fails AND we matched the ESC [ < prefix, scan forward up to a reasonable limit (e.g., 32 bytes — the max length of an SGR mouse sequence) for a terminating 'M' or 'm'. If found, it's malformed (parse would have succeeded if valid) — skip the non-matching bytes. If NOT found, it's truncated — return as partial. Also add a test: TestFilterMouse_MalformedSequenceDoesNotPoisonStream that feeds \x1b[<BOGUS through filterMouseForStatusBar, then verifies subsequent normal bytes are forwarded correctly (not buffered into carry).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: /strict-review-gate produces two contiguous PASS reviews. Both confirm: (1) Malformed SGR sequences (\x1b[<BOGUS) are NOT treated as incomplete — they are skipped/forwarded rather than buffered in the carry, (2) Truncated sequences at buffer boundary (\x1b[<1;2) ARE correctly buffered as partial, (3) TestFilterMouse_MalformedSequenceDoesNotPoisonStream passes — normal input works after malformed sequence, (4) All existing sgrmouse tests pass, (5) gmake lint and gmake deadcode pass.

sequentialTasks.task: 8R6: Fix manager — emit EventSessionActivated in handleRestoreState + fix sortSessionIDs overflow
sequentialTasks.description: REVIEW-5 #2 #3. Two fixes in internal/termmux/: (1) persistence.go handleRestoreState (line 247-249): after setting m.activeID = newActiveID, emit m.eventBus.emit(EventSessionActivated, newActiveID) so UI consumers (TUI status bar) are notified of the active session change. Also update ms.lastActive for the activated session. (2) manager.go sortSessionIDs (line 1052-1053): fix integer overflow in `int(b - a)` for uint64 SessionID values. Replace with `cmp.Compare(b, a)` which is overflow-safe. Import "cmp" package. TDD: (1) Add TestRestoreFromState_EmitsActivatedEvent that creates a manager, restores state with an active ID, and verifies EventSessionActivated is received via the event bus. (2) Add TestSortSessionIDs_NoOverflow with uint64 values near MaxUint64 to verify no panic or incorrect ordering.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: /strict-review-gate produces two contiguous PASS reviews. Both confirm: (1) handleRestoreState emits EventSessionActivated when activeID is set during restoration, (2) sortSessionIDs uses cmp.Compare for overflow-safe comparison, (3) TestRestoreFromState_EmitsActivatedEvent passes, (4) TestSortSessionIDs_NoOverflow passes with extreme uint64 values, (5) All existing manager/persistence tests pass, (6) gmake lint and gmake deadcode pass, (7) import of "cmp" is used (no unused import).

sequentialTasks.task: 8R7: Fix module.go — renderRaster: temp file leak + CR handling + dimension validation + predictable path
sequentialTasks.description: REVIEW-6 #1 #4 + REVIEW-7 #3 #4 #5. File: internal/builtin/termmux/module.go, function renderRaster (lines 904-967). FIVE fixes: (1) Temp file leak (REVIEW-6 #1 + REVIEW-7 #5): replace filepath.Join(os.TempDir(), fmt.Sprintf(...)) with os.CreateTemp. Use os.CreateTemp(tmpDir, "osm-raster-*.png"), write the PNG to the created file, then return the name. This gives atomic creation, unique names, and avoids predictable-path collisions. (2) CR handling (REVIEW-6 #4 + REVIEW-7 #3): in the PlainText character loop, add `if ch == '\r' { col = 0; continue }` to handle \r\n sequences correctly. \r resets column without advancing row; subsequent \n advances row. (3) Dimension validation (REVIEW-7 #4): after parsing cellW/cellH from options, validate: `if cellW <= 0 || cellH <= 0 { panic(runtime.NewTypeError("renderRaster: cell dimensions must be positive")) }`. (4) Remove the filename collision risk entirely by using os.CreateTemp. (5) Use the returned file for SaveRasterPNG — note that SaveRasterPNG calls os.Create internally, so we need to adjust: either write directly to the temp file or pass the path to SaveRasterPNG. Since CreateTemp already creates the file, we should write directly using png.Encode. TDD: add TestRenderRaster_CarriageReturn handling, TestRenderRaster_InvalidDimensions panic, and verify the temp file pattern creates unique files.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: /strict-review-gate produces two contiguous PASS reviews. Both confirm: (1) os.CreateTemp is used for unique, secure temp file creation, (2) \r resets col to 0 without advancing row, (3) cellW <= 0 or cellH <= 0 panics with clear error, (4) no predictable path vulnerability, (5) existing renderRaster tests pass, (6) gmake lint and gmake deadcode pass.

sequentialTasks.task: 8R8: Fix module.go — restoreState: nil-guard sessionsObj.ToObject + guard Target.Name + guard f.Error.Error() + consistent array parsing
sequentialTasks.description: REVIEW-6 #2 #3 + REVIEW-7 #1 #2 #6. File: internal/builtin/termmux/module.go, function restoreState (lines 1606-1721). FOUR fixes: (1) Nil guard on sessionsObj.ToObject (REVIEW-6 #2): after `sessionsObj.Get(key)`, check if the result is null/undefined before calling `.ToObject(runtime)`. If nil, skip that session entry. (2) Guard Target.Name (REVIEW-7 #1): `targetObj.Get("name")` may return goja Undefined, which `.String()` converts to literal string "undefined". Guard with `!goja.IsUndefined(v)` check before assignment. (3) Guard f.Error.Error() (REVIEW-7 #2): when mapping failed restorations, f.Error could theoretically be nil. Use a defensive helper: `func() string { if f.Error != nil { return f.Error.Error() }; return "" }()`. (4) Consistent args array parsing (REVIEW-7 #6): replace `argsObj.Keys()` iteration with index-based iteration matching newCaptureSession pattern: check `.length` and iterate 0..length-1 using `fmt.Sprintf("%d", i)` for key lookup. This is more robust against sparse arrays and non-index properties. TDD: add TestRestoreState_NullSessionEntry, TestRestoreState_MissingTargetName, TestRestoreState_NilErrorInFailed.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: /strict-review-gate produces two contiguous PASS reviews. Both confirm: (1) sessionsObj.ToObject is nil-guarded — null/undefined session values are skipped, (2) Target.Name uses goja.IsUndefined guard — missing 'name' key does not produce literal "undefined", (3) f.Error.Error() is nil-guarded — nil error in failed result doesn't panic, (4) args array parsing uses index-based iteration matching newCaptureSession, (5) new edge case tests pass, (6) gmake lint and gmake deadcode pass.

sequentialTasks.task: 8R9: Cross-platform verification: ALL review fixes pass full gauntlet on macOS, Linux, Windows
sequentialTasks.description: After ALL 8 review-fix tasks (8R1-8R8) have individually passed strict-review-gate and been committed, run the full verification gauntlet across all three platforms: (1) macOS: gmake make-all-with-log, (2) Linux container: gmake make-all-in-container, (3) Windows: gmake make-all-run-windows. Also run full (non-short) test suite. Record all results in WIP.md.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All review-fix commits pass full build+test+lint+deadcode on macOS, Linux container, and Windows. gmake cross-build succeeds. gmake deadcode reports zero unreachable functions. WIP.md updated with cross-platform results.

sequentialTasks.acceptance: The rastertest package compiles and exports all harness functions. TestHarness_SpawnEcho passes on macOS, skips on unsupported platforms. gmake lint and gmake deadcode pass. gmake cross-build passes.

sequentialTasks.description: Three visual raster tests: TestRaster_BasicText, TestRaster_ANSIColor, TestRaster_Scrolling. Uses rastertest harness. Full spec in old task 10. All use testing.Short() skip guards.

sequentialTasks.task: 8R14: Public module readiness audit: verify zero OSM-internal imports, export review (renumbered from old task 13)
sequentialTasks.description: Run go list -deps ./internal/termmux/... and verify ALL deps are external. Search for product-specific strings. Review exported APIs for naming conventions. Verify doc comments. Full spec in old task 13.

continuousVerification.failPolicy: If ANY test fails on ANY platform, STOP IMMEDIATELY and fix before proceeding.

finalEnforcementProtocol.alert: REVIEW GATE: Rule of Two — two contiguous PASS reviews on identical diff before any commit. Counter resets on code change.
DEADCODE: gmake deadcode must report zero unreachable functions. Wire up or delete — never .deadcodeignore.
CROSS-PLATFORM: ALL checks must pass on macOS, Linux, AND Windows.
TDD: Write failing test FIRST. Confirm it fails. Then implement the fix.
SKIP: Breaking API changes (Dirty field removal, exported field changes) are EXCLUDED — all Go code is internal-only.
